questions.q: In which Indian city is the Lotus Temple located?
questions.explanation: The Lotus Temple stands in New Delhi, in the Bahapur area of the national capital.

questions.q: The Lotus Temple is a house of worship for which faith?
questions.explanation: It is a Bahai House of Worship, one of several major Bahai temples around the world.

questions.q: What flower is the shape of the Lotus Temple based on?
questions.explanation: The building is designed to resemble a half-open lotus flower, giving it its popular name.

questions.q: In what year was the Lotus Temple opened to the public?
questions.explanation: The Lotus Temple was completed and dedicated in 1986.

questions.q: How many petals make up the structure of the Lotus Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple is formed of 27 free-standing marble-clad petals arranged in clusters of three.

questions.q: Who is welcome to enter and pray inside the Lotus Temple?
questions.explanation: Like all Bahai Houses of Worship, the Lotus Temple is open to people of every religion and background.

questions.q: What is the main material used for the exterior cladding of the Lotus Temple?
questions.explanation: The petals are clad in white marble, giving the temple its luminous appearance.

questions.q: The Lotus Temple is often grouped with other Bahai temples around the world known by what collective term?
questions.explanation: Bahai temples are formally called Houses of Worship, and the Lotus Temple is one of them.

questions.q: What kind of activity primarily takes place inside the central hall of the Lotus Temple?
questions.explanation: The central prayer hall is used for quiet prayer, meditation, and the reading of scriptures from various faiths.

questions.q: Which of these is true about the colour scheme of the Lotus Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple is famous for its pristine white marble petals set amid green gardens and pools.

questions.q: Which architect designed the Lotus Temple?
questions.explanation: The Iranian-Canadian architect Fariborz Sahba designed the Lotus Temple.

questions.q: Bahai Houses of Worship are required to have how many sides or entrances symbolically?
questions.explanation: Bahai temples are nine-sided, and the Lotus Temple has nine doors opening onto a central hall.

questions.q: How many pools surround the Lotus Temple, echoing the lotus floating on water?
questions.explanation: Nine reflecting pools surround the temple, suggesting the floating leaves of a lotus.

questions.q: The 27 petals of the Lotus Temple are arranged in clusters of how many?
questions.explanation: The 27 petals are grouped into nine clusters of three, forming the outer leaves, entrance petals, and inner petals.

questions.q: Roughly how many people can the central prayer hall of the Lotus Temple seat?
questions.explanation: The central hall can accommodate around 1,300 people.

questions.q: Which sacred number recurs throughout the Lotus Temple's design (sides, doors, pools)?
questions.explanation: The number nine, significant in the Bahai Faith, appears in the nine sides, nine doors, and nine pools.

questions.q: The white marble used to clad the Lotus Temple's petals was imported from which country?
questions.explanation: The petals are clad in white Pentelic marble imported from Greece, set on the concrete shells.

questions.q: Bahai Houses of Worship are dedicated to the unity of what?
options: God and all religions and humanity
questions.explanation: Bahai temples express the principle of the oneness of God, religion, and humanity, welcoming all people.

questions.q: What feature is notably absent from worship inside the Lotus Temple, as in all Bahai temples?
questions.explanation: Bahai worship has no idols, images, pulpit-based sermons, or ritual ceremonies; only scriptures are read or chanted.

questions.q: Approximately how tall is the Lotus Temple structure?
questions.explanation: The temple rises to roughly 34 metres in height from the base to the top of the inner petals.

questions.q: The Lotus Temple has become famous partly for what visitor-related distinction?
options: Being the smallest temple in India
Being among the most-visited buildings in the world
questions.explanation: It has drawn enormous numbers of visitors, making it one of the most-visited buildings in the world.

questions.q: What is the regional role of the Lotus Temple within the Bahai Faith?
options: It is the mother temple of Africa
It serves as the Bahai House of Worship for the Indian subcontinent
It is located in South America
questions.explanation: The Lotus Temple serves as the Bahai House of Worship for the Indian subcontinent.

questions.q: In which decade was the Lotus Temple built before its 1986 completion?
questions.explanation: Planning and construction spanned the late 1970s into the early 1980s, culminating in the temple's 1986 dedication.

questions.q: Which structural approach was used for the petals of the Lotus Temple?
questions.explanation: The petals are reinforced-concrete shells engineered into a self-supporting form, then clad with white marble.

questions.q: Which UK firm of consulting engineers was associated with the structural engineering of the Lotus Temple?
questions.explanation: The UK firm Flint and Neill served as structural consultants for the temple's complex shell geometry.

questions.q: The three sets of petals in the Lotus Temple are commonly described by which names?
questions.explanation: The petals are grouped as entrance leaves, outer leaves, and inner leaves, each cluster of nine forming the lotus shape.

questions.q: For what was the Lotus Temple notably recognised by architecture and engineering bodies?
options: Its innovative structural and architectural design
Being the cheapest building of its era
questions.explanation: The temple won several architecture and engineering honours, including recognition for its innovative structural design.

questions.q: The Lotus Temple is sometimes compared to the Sydney Opera House because both are known for what?
options: Sculptural shell-like roof forms inspired by nature
Being the tallest buildings on their continents
questions.explanation: Both are celebrated for expressive, sculptural shell forms; the Lotus Temple's petals echo a flower as the Opera House's shells evoke sails.

questions.q: Energy and sustainability efforts at the Lotus Temple have included which notable installation?
options: Solar power panels to supply part of its electricity
questions.explanation: The temple installed solar panels, making it one of the early major Indian landmarks to generate part of its own electricity from solar power.

questions.q: What is the architectural significance of the Lotus Temple's curved petal shells lacking straight load-bearing walls?
options: It means the roof is removable
The curved shells themselves carry the loads, a hallmark of shell architecture
questions.explanation: The doubly curved marble-clad concrete shells act as the structure, exemplifying mid-to-late 20th-century shell and expressionist architecture.
